execution_plan.reasoning: DECOMPOSE: find zones with lights off, bins full, and traffic problems (critical congestion or incidents) | MAP: smart-lighting-mock / GET /light, smart-bins-mock / GET /bin, smart-traffic-monitoring-mock / GET /traffic-sensor | CHAIN: SQL join over three GET results on zoneId | COMBINE: sql join get_lights_off and get_bins_full and get_traffic_problems | FILTER: status=off for lights, status=full for bins, congestionLevel=critical OR incidentDetected=true for traffic | VALIDATE: ✓ three GETs + terminal SQL, ✓ no invented thresholds, ✓ no AND-combined params

question: The city feels chaotic today. Where is there heavy congestion, delayed public transport, and few emergency units available?

execution_plan.reasoning: DECOMPOSE: find zones with heavy congestion, delayed transport, and low emergency unit availability | MAP: smart-traffic-monitoring-mock / GET /traffic-sensor (congestionLevel=critical), smart-public-transport-mock / GET /vehicle (status=delayed), smart-emergency-services-mock / GET /emergency-unit (available=false) | CHAIN: SQL join/aggregate over three GET results | COMBINE: sql join task_traffic_sensors, task_delayed_vehicles, task_unavailable_units to find intersecting zones and rank by severity | FILTER: congestionLevel=critical, status=delayed, available=false (catalog enums) | VALIDATE: ✓

input.sql_query: SELECT t.zoneId, COUNT(DISTINCT t.id) as congestion_count, COUNT(DISTINCT v.id) as delayed_count, COUNT(DISTINCT u.id) as unavailable_count FROM task_traffic_sensors t JOIN task_delayed_vehicles v ON t.zoneId = v.zoneId JOIN task_unavailable_units u ON t.zoneId = u.zoneId GROUP BY t.zoneId ORDER BY congestion_count DESC, delayed_count DESC, unavailable_count DESC

question: I want somewhere calm to go, with something to do and a place to park. What would you suggest?

execution_plan.reasoning: DECOMPOSE: find quiet zones (low noise), find open attractions in those zones, find free parking in those zones | MAP: smart-environment-and-temperature-sensors-mock (noise sensors), smart-tourist-attractions-mock (attractions), smart-parking-mock (parking) | CHAIN: SQL join over three datasets | COMBINE: sql join get_noise_sensors, get_open_attractions, get_free_parking on zoneId, order by noise level | FILTER: noise sensors (sensorType=noise), attractions (status=open), parking (status=free) | VALIDATE: ✓

input.sql_query: SELECT n.zoneId, n.lastReading AS noiseLevel, a.name AS attraction, a.category, p.location AS parkingLocation FROM get_noise_sensors n JOIN get_open_attractions a ON n.zoneId = a.zoneId JOIN get_free_parking p ON n.zoneId = p.zoneId ORDER BY n.lastReading ASC LIMIT 5
